mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 3.5% (CPIAUCSL) signals central bank inaction, leading to real rate compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite inflationary signal, or CPIAUCSL declines below 2.5% without corresponding equity gains.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates robust demand conditions, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings prospects.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of periods where both GDPC1 and HOUST show positive growth, or the correlation between proxies and returns drops below 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening labor market (UNRATE < 4%) combined with strong payroll growth (PAYEMS > 150k) signals inflationary wage pressures, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the joint condition fails to correlate with returns (Rank IC < 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO < 102.5) during a supply-side shock indicates reduced corporate capacity utilization, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO declines below 102.5, or the signal's Rank IC falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending (RSAFS > 700B) during low unemployment (UNRATE < 4%) indicates strong demand-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the joint condition's predictive power drops below 0.15 (Rank IC).

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 5%) during declining GDP growth (GDPC1 < 2%) signals a tightening cycle, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the signal's Rank IC falls below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO < 102) during rising inflation (CPIAUCSL > 3.2%) indicates stagflationary pressures, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the joint condition's predictive power drops below 0.1 (Rank IC).
